political.s: <cite index="1-1,1-2">Campbell's is committed to fair labor practices and sustainable sourcing, working with suppliers on environmental performance and human rights standards.</cite> Government FEC data shows the company leans bipartisan with low-level political donations of $4K, comprising 57% Republican and 43% Democratic contributions, reflecting minimal engagement in federal politics.

charity.s: <cite index="12-3,12-4">Since 1953, The Campbell's Foundation has supported local communities through grants and partnerships, with three main focus areas: increasing food access, encouraging healthy living, and nurturing neighborhoods.</cite> <cite index="16-20,16-21">In recent years, the foundation gave approximately $1.4 million in grants, with more than half supporting organizations fighting food insecurity.</cite>
charity.amt: $1-1.4 million annually (foundation grants); additional corporate/employee giving programs
charity.focus: Food access, hunger relief, healthy living, community development

environment.s: <cite index="74-6">Campbell committed to reduce absolute Scope 1 and 2 greenhouse gas emissions 42% by fiscal 2030 from a fiscal 2020 base year.</cite> <cite index="73-3,73-4">The company was named one of the 100 Most Sustainable Corporations in the World by Corporate Knights, and is one of only two U.S. food companies included in the Global 100.</cite> However, <cite index="76-7">Campbell receives criticism for using genetically modified ingredients.</cite>

labor.s: <cite index="33-8,33-9">San Diego filed a lawsuit against Campbell and subsidiaries for misclassifying hundreds of California workers as independent contractors in its direct-store-delivery model for product stocking and merchandising.</cite> Additionally, <cite index="37-1">Campbell faced a WARN Act investigation for potentially failing to provide 60 days' notice before laying off 205 employees in Paris, Texas.</cite> <cite index="38-2,38-3">A former cybersecurity analyst sued for being fired after reporting a senior executive's derogatory remarks about the company's products and Indian employees.</cite>

dei.s: <cite index="21-18,21-19">Campbell increased I&D learning opportunities and by 2021, 96% of people managers completed I&D training.</cite> <cite index="24-3,24-7">The company pledged $1.5 million over three years to nonprofit organizations fighting racism and discrimination.</cite> <cite index="29-2,29-3">Campbell's DEI rating on Glassdoor is 3.9 out of 5 stars, equal to the average for DEI ratings in the Manufacturing sector.</cite>

animals.s: Not applicable—Campbell Soup is a food processing company that does not conduct animal testing of products or ingredients.

guns.s: Not applicable—Campbell Soup is a food and beverage manufacturer with no involvement in firearms sales or manufacturing.

privacy.s: <cite index="61-5,61-6">Campbell's privacy policy states the company has created its policy to provide transparency about information collection, handling, storage, and protection, and how customers can control certain uses of information.</cite> <cite index="69-2,69-3">Campbell does not sell personal information and does not share personal information for cross-contextual behavioral advertising purposes.</cite>

execPay.s: <cite index="55-2">Campbell paid its CEO total annual compensation of US$12 million over the year to July 2024, a 4.8% increase over the previous year.</cite> <cite index="55-15,55-16">Over three years, Campbell Soup shrunk earnings per share by 17% annually while revenue increased 3%.</cite>

fec.committeeName: CAMPBELL SOUP COMPANY PAC CAMPBELL PAC

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
